A test script for verifying a function that returns its argument.

# Purpose
This code defines a simple JavaScript function named [`foo`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/13/13-013foo>) that takes a single parameter called `arguments` and returns it unchanged. The function provides narrow functionality, serving as a basic identity function that returns its input. Following the function definition, there is an assertion using the `assert` function to verify that calling [`foo`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/13/13-013foo>) with the argument `1` returns `1`, confirming the expected behavior of the [`foo`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/13/13-013foo>) function. This code is a short script that demonstrates a basic function and its test case.

### foo<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/13/13-013.foo}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/13/13-013.js#L15>)

Returns the input argument as the output.
- **Inputs**:
    - `arguments`: The input to the function, which can be of any type.
- **Control Flow**:
    - The function receives an input argument.
    - The function returns the input argument without modification.
- **Output**: The same value as the input argument.
